task_language: pick up the book and place it in the back compartment of the caddy
strategy_notes: Single-attempt success by replaying the proven r6 recipe (3 prior rounds — r4, r5, r6 — all solved this exact cell with the same approach). Scene (agentview hi-res image_cam_hi_00.png): wooden desk caddy on the table-left at world x~[-0.45,-0.32] with multiple compartments including a back-middle one; the black book stands upright on the table in the center-foreground at world ~(-0.06,-0.01,0.97); a white-yellow mug sits on the table-right (irrelevant). Perception: verified the book center via world_hi_00.npy pixel probes (cols ~480-510, rows ~750-820) returning x in [-0.085,-0.05], y in [-0.02,-0.001], z in [0.94,1.02] — matches the deterministic s0 scene reported in r4/r5/r6, so the proven xyz coords transfer directly. Recipe: (1) move_to (-0.06,-0.01,1.10) gripper -1 to pre-position over the upright book; (2) pi0_pick 'pick up the black book' succeeded in 4 chunks (peak_lift=0.0758m, gripper closed to 0.0316m — book held vertically); (3) set_gripper +1 (10 steps) to firm the grip before carrying; (4) waypoint move_to (-0.20,0,1.20) gripper 1 to keep single-step xy < 0.30 and clear caddy dividers; (5) move_to (-0.42,-0.14,1.20) gripper 1 over the back-middle compartment center; (6) move_to (-0.42,-0.14,1.05) gripper 1 — OSC vertical-down wall stalled the eef at z~1.15 (final_dist 0.10), but with the book hanging ~15cm below the gripper its bottom is already inside the compartment; (7) release (10 steps); (8) move_to (-0.30,-0.14,1.25) gripper -1 to retreat — libero_terminated fired on step 8. KEY LEVERS: (a) BACK-MIDDLE compartment (x=-0.42, y=-0.14) is the correct 'back compartment' for this caddy variant (NOT left-large nor right-back, per r5/r6 forensics); (b) gripper:+1 must be passed in every carry move_to AND firmed with set_gripper +1 right after pi0_pick (omitting it silently opens the gripper); (c) the OSC vertical wall at z~1.15 is acceptable — the book hangs below the grip so it falls the remaining ~10cm into the compartment on release; (d) no tilt/move_pose needed — straight vertical descent into the wall + release is enough because the held book extends the effective reach.

place_result.note: OSC vertical-down wall at z~1.15 — book hanging below grip dropped into the back-middle compartment on release
